project.name: qq
project.description: qq is a bespoke, single-operator agentic-development command center that layers curated invocable skills, operating rules, an auto-generated code-knowledge graph, parallel named agent sessions, and a tuned terminal cockpit over a thin surface of external tools. It is capability the operator reaches for rather than process to maintain, with Markdown operating docs and Bash scripts as its primary substrate.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: Model Context Protocol configuration registering the Context7 server (live, version-correct library docs), launched on demand via npx.

nodes.name: .no-mistakes.yaml
nodes.summary: Repo configuration for qq's externally-owned no-mistakes merge gate: sets the pipeline agent (codex), the shellcheck lint command over the bin/ scripts, intent checking, and out-of-tree evidence handling for the blast-radius gate.
nodes.languageNotes: agent + commands are read from the default branch as a no-mistakes security rule, so the file only takes effect once committed to main.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Root operating-rules header loaded every session: defines qq's purpose, the blast-radius merge gate, and @-imports the shared methodology; CLAUDE.md symlinks to it.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Symlink to AGENTS.md so Claude Code loads the same operating-rules header under its expected filename; not a separate document.
nodes.languageNotes: Symlink to AGENTS.md — identical content, Claude-Code-specific filename.

nodes.name: CONCEPTS.md
nodes.summary: Durable domain-vocabulary ledger appended by the ce-compound skill and read by agents to share precise, project-specific terminology across sessions; currently a template stub.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and setup guide describing qq's six layers, the develop loop, the curated skill set, and step-by-step activation, linking out to AGENTS.md, qq-methodology.md, and SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md.

nodes.name: SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md
nodes.summary: Provenance and license attribution tables for qq's skill set, distinguishing the MIT-vendored skills from the four authored or synthesized specifically for qq.

nodes.name: qq-methodology.md
nodes.summary: The shared operating core imported live by every qq-linked repo: defines the six layers, the behavioral floor, task routing, the seven-stage loop, git landing rules, merge-gate modes, and the skill index.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: qq preflight script that inspects the external tool surface (ripgrep, fd, eza, gh, herdr, cockpit tools, knowledge graph, Context7, skills) and prints exact, package-manager-aware setup steps without installing anything.
nodes.languageNotes: Read-only by design: it probes with `command -v` and emits brew/apt hints rather than installing, so it is safe to re-run idempotently.

nodes.name: qq-activate.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot, idempotent activation of the qq setup: installs herdr integrations, symlinks cockpit configs into ~/.config, links skills via qq-link.sh, installs the git rail plus the wip-savepoint Stop hook, flips Claude Code and Codex into yolo mode, then commits and pushes the link artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Installs the destructive-git rail BEFORE enabling bypassPermissions, and mixes Bash with an inline Python heredoc to edit ~/.claude/settings.json idempotently while backing up each edited file to *.qq.bak.

nodes.name: qq-link.sh
nodes.summary: CLI that links the qq methodology and skills live from this repo: `skills` symlinks every skill into ~/.claude/skills, and `repo <path>` wires a target repo to the shared methodology (symlink, AGENTS.md import, Context7 MCP merge, CONCEPTS.md seed) with a validated merge-gate option.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds a Python heredoc to merge the context7 entry into a repo's .mcp.json (avoiding a hard jq dependency); all link operations are idempotent, backing up pre-existing real files to numbered *.qq.bak names.

nodes.name: qq-wip-snapshot.sh
nodes.summary: Non-destructive savepoint hook run on every Claude Code Stop: snapshots the current working tree (tracked + untracked) into refs/wip/<branch> without touching HEAD, the index, or any file, no-oping when clean, outside a repo, or unchanged since the last snapshot.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a temporary GIT_INDEX_FILE with git read-tree/add/write-tree then commit-tree + update-ref to record the snapshot as a parented commit under refs/wip/<branch> — a purely additive git plumbing pattern that never disturbs the real index or HEAD.

nodes.name: backup_path
nodes.summary: Renames an existing path to a non-colliding *.qq.bak backup, appending an incrementing numeric suffix until a free name is found.

nodes.name: link_one
nodes.summary: Idempotently symlinks src to dst: reports and returns if the correct link already exists, removes a stale symlink, backs up a pre-existing real file, then creates the symlink.

nodes.name: link_skills
nodes.summary: Symlinks every skill directory under the repo's skills/ into ~/.claude/skills and prints the count linked.

nodes.name: ensure_agents_import
nodes.summary: Ensures a target repo's AGENTS.md imports the shared methodology — scaffolding a new file with the merge gate when absent, or appending the @.claude/qq-methodology.md import when missing.

nodes.name: merge_context7
nodes.summary: Idempotently merges the context7 MCP server entry into a repo's .mcp.json via an embedded Python heredoc, preserving existing content and adding the server only when absent.

nodes.name: seed_concepts
nodes.summary: Creates a starter CONCEPTS.md vocabulary file with a header explaining its purpose when one does not already exist.

nodes.name: link_repo
nodes.summary: Wires a target repo to the shared qq methodology: parses and validates the --gate option, symlinks the methodology file, ensures the AGENTS.md import, merges Context7, and seeds CONCEPTS.md.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point dispatching the skills, repo, and help subcommands to their handlers, printing usage and exiting non-zero on unknown input.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-link-not-copy-methodology.md
nodes.summary: Two-handoff implementation plan to make qq the single source of truth for the agent methodology by replacing hand-copied AGENTS.md and the plugin-copied skills with live symlinked imports of a new shared `qq-methodology.md` core, eliminating drift.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured as sequential Codex handoffs with explicit per-handoff acceptance criteria and an owner-only manual step; working-tree-only, no auto-commit.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-no-mistakes-gate-integration.md
nodes.summary: Five-task plan to adopt the external no-mistakes gate as the repo's blast-radius merge gate — trialing it in a scratch clone (GO/NO-GO), installing it, rewriting AGENTS.md to describe the layered author-side vs verifier-side skill split, dogfooding a real PR, and compounding the decision.
nodes.languageNotes: Long checkbox-driven plan with heavy global constraints framing a hard GO/NO-GO gate at Task 1; describes the gate's fixed pipeline (intent → rebase → review → test → document → lint → push → pr → ci).

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-no-mistakes-trial-report.md
nodes.summary: Trial report and GO verdict from running the no-mistakes gate end-to-end in an isolated scratch clone with a local bare upstream, recording the init manifest, pipeline result, an auto-fixed lint bug, cost/latency, and safety confirmations.
nodes.languageNotes: Companion evidence artifact for the gate-integration plan's Task 1 GO/NO-GO gate; records that the real remote was never touched.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-qq-ac-reframe.md
nodes.summary: Phased plan to rename the repo from hypercore to qq-ac — applying a verbatim naming map across identifiers, adding a sixth Cockpit layer that vendors tuned tool configs via symlinks, then landing through the gate and flipping the GitHub/on-disk names last.
nodes.languageNotes: Three concentric phases sequenced so the risky live-session cwd/GitHub rename happens only after the in-repo transform lands; includes a token-for-token naming map table.

nodes.name: 01-btw-ideas-skill.md
nodes.summary: Design note proposing a `/btw` mid-session idea-capture skill that sharpens and researches a dropped thought in the background and files it ready to take on; design is aligned with three open decisions but not yet built.

nodes.name: 02-orchestrate-phase-state.md
nodes.summary: Note on a built-but-unmerged `qq-phase` feature that stamps the orchestrate loop's current phase to `.orchestrate/state.json` for a status widget; salvaged from a pruned branch as a re-appliable patch pending a decision to promote or drop.

nodes.name: 03-codex-exec-stdin-hang.md
nodes.summary: Bug note documenting that `codex exec` blocks forever reading stdin unless it is closed, with the one-line fix `< /dev/null` and where to wire it so it never recurs.

nodes.name: 04-gate-stale-path-after-rename.md
nodes.summary: Bug note explaining why `git push no-mistakes` silently opens no PR after a repo rename (the gate's stored repo path is stale) and that `no-mistakes init` repairs it; also records a second open failure mode where the run stalls right after review.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index and grooming guide for the informal ideas backlog, with one-line pointers to each numbered idea/bug note and inline quick-thought bullets.

nodes.name: qq-herdr-pull
nodes.summary: Extension-less Bash CLI that pulls a herdr agent into the operator's focused pane (via pane move + close), selecting by 1-based index or `next` (most-needy: blocked > working > idle).
nodes.languageNotes: Strict-mode Bash (set -euo pipefail) that surfaces failures as herdr toasts rather than hard errors; guards jq up front and rejects non-positive indices to avoid jq's negative-index wraparound.

nodes.name: qq-wip
nodes.summary: Bash CLI to inspect and recover working-tree savepoints under refs/wip/<branch> — list, diff against HEAD, or materialize the latest snapshot as a fresh branch. Recovery is always non-destructive.

nodes.name: glow.yml
nodes.summary: Glow defaults for in-pane Markdown rendering — fixed width, no pager, mouse off — pointing at the hand-tuned theme file.

nodes.name: tuned.json
nodes.summary: Hand-tuned Glow Markdown theme defining colors and styling for every element (headings, code blocks, links, tables, lists) used when rendering docs in-pane.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Herdr cockpit configuration — tokyo-night theme, agent sorting, and custom pane keybindings (prefix+f runs qqy, prefix+shift+f runs qqbr, plus detached keys that invoke qq-herdr-pull).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Overview of the cockpit terminal surface — the source of truth for each config symlinked into ~/.config — with a file inventory and the yazi/glow/herdr navigation flow.

nodes.name: file-navigation.bash
nodes.summary: Sourced shell helpers for cockpit navigation — generic y()/br() wrappers around yazi and broot that cd to the chosen directory, plus repo-focused qqroot/qqy/qqbr and qfiles/qtree aliases.

nodes.name: keymap.toml
nodes.summary: Yazi keymap overrides — `!` opens a shell in the current directory and `g H` jumps to the qq project root.

nodes.name: yazi.toml
nodes.summary: Yazi file-manager config for pane-first navigation — Markdown opens in-pane via mdcat by default with tuned Glow as the alternate opener.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index note for the docs/solutions/ directory where the ce-compound skill records durable solution write-ups from solved problems.

nodes.name: 02-orchestrate-phase-state.patch
nodes.summary: Captured unified-diff proposal to add phase-state tracking to the orchestrate skill — an idea artifact recording a stalled post-review gate design rather than applied code.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: ce-compound skill definition — after a solved problem, produce two artifacts: a dated solution doc under docs/solutions/ and durable vocabulary in CONCEPTS.md, with a quality bar for each.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: code-review skill definition — pin a fixed point, then spawn parallel Standards and Spec sub-agents to review a diff along both axes and aggregate their findings side by side.

nodes.name: hitl-loop.template.sh
nodes.summary: Copy-and-edit human-in-the-loop reproduction template — step()/capture() helpers prompt the user through manual steps and print captured KEY=VALUE answers for the agent to parse.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: diagnosing-bugs skill definition — a six-phase loop (build a feedback loop, reproduce/minimise, hypothesise, instrument, fix + regression test, cleanup/post-mortem) for hard bugs and perf regressions.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: executing-plans skill definition — load a written plan, execute tasks with review checkpoints, and stop to ask for help on blockers rather than touching main without consent.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: finishing-a-development-branch skill definition — verify tests, detect environment and base branch, then present merge / PR / keep / discard options and execute the chosen path with cleanup.

nodes.name: block-dangerous-git.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code PreToolUse(Bash) hook that reads the command and exits 2 to block destructive git — force-push, reset --hard, clean -f, branch -D, checkout/restore ., and history rewrites — while allowing plain git push.
nodes.languageNotes: Matches a Bash array of extended-regex danger patterns against the JSON tool input parsed with jq; narrowed from the upstream MIT skill to force-pushes only so agents can still ship.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: git-guardrails skill definition — installs the block-dangerous-git.sh hook into Claude Code settings, documenting what gets blocked and the copy/configure/verify setup steps.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: grill-me skill definition — a short trigger-phrase alias that routes into the grilling skill to stress-test a plan or design.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: grilling skill definition — relentlessly interrogate a plan or design to pin down intent and surface open decision branches before building.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: handoff skill definition — compact the current session state into a concise brief so a fresh agent can resume when the context window fills.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: orchestrate skill definition — conduct a non-trivial task through qq's full loop as a two-model split (Claude conducts/judges, Codex implements) across triage, align, plan, build, verify, sign-off, review, and compound.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: receiving-code-review skill definition — weigh review feedback with technical rigor instead of rubber-stamping, covering response patterns, forbidden responses, when to push back, and source-specific handling.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: research skill definition — investigate a question against primary sources and Context7, adversarially verify each claim, and leave one cited, confidence-tagged Markdown file in research/.

nodes.name: y
nodes.summary: Launches yazi with a temp cwd-file and, on exit, cd's the shell to the directory the user navigated to.

nodes.name: br
nodes.summary: Launches broot and applies its emitted cd command so the shell follows the directory chosen in the tree.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the uat-signoff skill: the human-acceptance layer that walks the owner through one observable test at a time after autonomous verification, inferring gap severity from the owner's own words. Fires selectively on user-facing, irreversible, or ambiguous changes.
nodes.languageNotes: Skill spec with reply-classification and severity-inference tables; explicitly composes on top of verification-before-completion as the machine gate.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the verification-before-completion skill: the never-skipped rule requiring fresh command evidence before any completion, passing, or fixed claim. Provides the gate function, common-failure and rationalization tables, and red-flag stop conditions.
nodes.languageNotes: Prohibition-style skill built around an 'Iron Law' and a numbered gate function; the load-bearing verification step referenced by many other skills in the loop.

nodes.name: plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md
nodes.summary: Reusable subagent prompt template for the writing-plans skill: dispatches a plan-document reviewer that checks a finished plan for completeness, spec alignment, task decomposition, and buildability before implementation.
nodes.languageNotes: Companion helper prompt for writing-plans; embeds a calibration section that tells the reviewer to flag only real implementation blockers.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the writing-plans skill: how to turn a spec into a comprehensive, bite-sized, TDD-structured implementation plan with exact file paths, task interfaces, and a self-review pass, then hand off to an execution skill.
nodes.languageNotes: Structural-slot skill: mandates a plan-document header and per-task template, and routes execution to subagent-driven-development or executing-plans.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the writing-skills meta-skill: the authoring discipline for qq's own skills, fusing structural best-practices (progressive disclosure, matched degrees of freedom, no-op test, positive prompting) with a RED-GREEN-REFACTOR eval loop that proves each skill against a baseline before it ships.
nodes.languageNotes: Meta-skill with disable-model-invocation; three-layer process (eval, structure, judge) plus a six-failure-mode review checklist and token-budget rules for progressive disclosure.

layers.name: Rules
layers.description: The behavioral floor and navigation backbone loaded every session — the operating rules (AGENTS.md and its CLAUDE.md symlink), the shared qq-methodology.md core, and the top-level overview/vocabulary/attribution docs (README.md, CONCEPTS.md, SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md).

layers.name: Actions (Skills)
layers.description: qq's atomic capabilities under skills/ — one SKILL.md per skill (grilling, orchestrate, code-review, writing-plans, verification-before-completion, ...), plus their helper scripts and reviewer prompts.

layers.name: Cockpit
layers.description: The tuned terminal surface under cockpit/ — herdr/yazi/glow configs, the in-pane Markdown theme, and the shell navigation helpers, documented by its own README.

layers.name: Externals
layers.description: The thin outward-facing surface — the Context7 MCP registration (.mcp.json) and the no-mistakes merge-gate config (.no-mistakes.yaml).

layers.name: Setup & Bootstrap
layers.description: The bin/ scripts that stand qq up and keep it safe — preflight tool check, one-shot activation, skill linking, WIP snapshot/recovery, and the herdr pane-pull CLI.

layers.name: Planning & History
layers.description: The narrative record of decisions and pending work — the docs/ implementation plans and solution history plus the ideas/ idea/bug backlog and its salvaged orchestrate-phase patch.

tour.title: What qq Is
tour.description: Start here: README.md is the project's front door and its highest-reach document, linking out to every other cornerstone file. It frames qq as a bespoke, single-operator agentic-development command center and lays out the mental model the rest of the tour follows: six layers (Rules, Actions, Knowledge, Sessions, Cockpit, Externals), the develop loop (Align to Plan to Build to Verify to Sign-off to Review to Compound), and the step-by-step activation sequence. Read this first to get the map before we walk the territory.

tour.title: The Operating Rules
tour.description: These three files are the Rules layer, loaded into every agent session as the behavioral floor. AGENTS.md is the repo's own operating-rules header (it defines qq's purpose and the blast-radius merge gate), and it @-imports qq-methodology.md, the shared core that every qq-linked repo runs on: the six layers, task routing, the seven-stage loop, and git landing rules. CLAUDE.md is just a symlink to AGENTS.md so Claude Code and Codex load the identical rules under the filename each expects. These are the most depended-upon documents in the whole graph, which is why they come right after the overview.
tour.languageLesson: The '@'-import in AGENTS.md pulls qq-methodology.md in live rather than copying it, so the shared methodology has exactly one source of truth and linked repos never drift out of sync. CLAUDE.md being a symlink (not a duplicate) applies the same 'link, don't copy' principle at the file level.

tour.title: The Actions Layer: Skills
tour.description: Skills are qq's atomic capabilities, one SKILL.md per skill, each invoked by name as a slash command. Together these five trace the develop loop the README described: grilling pins down intent before any code is written (Align), writing-plans turns the agreed intent into an executable plan (Plan), verification-before-completion is the never-skipped rule that demands command evidence before any 'done' claim (Verify), and code-review checks a diff along Standards and Spec axes (Review). orchestrate is the conductor that runs a whole task through that loop as a two-model split: Claude judges while Codex implements. Skills are the 'capability I reach for, not process I maintain' idea made concrete.

tour.title: Setup & Bootstrap
tour.description: How does a skill from Step 3 actually reach ~/.claude/skills so an agent can invoke it? Through these bin/ scripts, the top fan-in code files in the repo. install.sh is a read-only preflight that inspects the external tool surface and prints exact setup steps without touching anything. qq-activate.sh is the one-shot, idempotent activation that wires everything up: it symlinks cockpit configs, installs the git rail and WIP hook, and calls qq-link.sh, the CLI that symlinks every skill live from this repo and wires other repos to the shared methodology. This is the 'link, don't copy' rule from Step 2 turned into running code.
tour.languageLesson: These Bash scripts are written to be idempotent — safe to re-run because each step checks for its own prior result before acting (symlink already present, hook already installed). Live symlinking instead of copying means editing a skill in this repo instantly updates every place it is linked, with no re-sync step.

tour.title: The Safety Rails
tour.description: qq lets agents run fast because a net catches mistakes. qq-wip-snapshot.sh is a Stop hook that fires every time an agent goes idle, snapshotting the working tree into refs/wip/<branch> without ever touching HEAD, the index, or your files, so in-flight work is never lost. qq-wip is the CLI that lists, diffs, and recovers those snapshots, always non-destructively. block-dangerous-git.sh (the git-guardrails hook installed by qq-activate.sh in Step 4) is a PreToolUse gate that blocks force-push, reset --hard, and clean before they run, while still allowing a plain push. Together they are the un-green counterpart to qq's commit-on-green discipline.
tour.languageLesson: A Claude Code hook is a script the harness runs at a lifecycle point: a Stop hook (qq-wip-snapshot.sh) runs when the agent finishes a turn, and a PreToolUse hook (block-dangerous-git.sh) runs before a tool call and can veto it by exiting non-zero. Hooks are how qq enforces behavior the model itself cannot be trusted to always follow.

tour.title: The Cockpit
tour.description: The Cockpit is the human-driven terminal surface, and cockpit/README.md is its source-of-truth index: every config here is symlinked into ~/.config by the activation script from Step 4. herdr/config.toml tunes the parallel-agent session manager (theme, agent sorting, and the pane keybindings that pull a needy agent into focus), yazi.toml and glow.yml set up pane-first file browsing with in-pane Markdown rendering, and file-navigation.bash adds the shell wrappers (qqy, qqbr, qqroot) that tie yazi and broot into quick jumps. This is the layer the operator actually looks at while the agents work.
tour.languageLesson: These configs use TOML and YAML — declarative formats where you state desired settings as key/value tables rather than write logic. Keeping the canonical copy in the repo and symlinking it into ~/.config means the operator's tuned environment is version-controlled and reproducible, not hand-edited in place.

tour.title: The Externals
tour.description: The Externals layer is deliberately thin: only the outside tools that earn their keep. .mcp.json registers Context7, a Model Context Protocol server that supplies live, version-correct library documentation on demand. .no-mistakes.yaml configures the externally-owned no-mistakes merge gate that AGENTS.md invoked in Step 2: it names the pipeline agent (codex), the shellcheck lint run over the bin/ scripts, and the intent check. Real work is pushed to this gate, which independently reviews the diff, runs the checks, and opens a PR, so 'green' becomes a fact the gate proves rather than one the agent merely asserts.
tour.languageLesson: The gate config is YAML: a declarative contract the external pipeline reads to know how to validate this repo. Model Context Protocol (MCP), configured in .mcp.json, is a standard for connecting an agent to external tool servers — here it launches Context7 on demand via npx, so no service runs until a session actually needs docs.

tour.title: Planning & History
tour.description: Finally, the narrative record of how qq got here and where it is going. The docs/plans/ files are executable implementation plans landed through the loop: link-not-copy-methodology.md is the plan that made qq the single source of truth you saw in Step 2, while no-mistakes-gate-integration.md and its companion trial-report.md are the plan and GO verdict behind the gate from Step 7. docs/solutions/README.md indexes the durable write-ups the ce-compound skill produces after a solved problem, and ideas/README.md grooms the informal idea and bug backlog. This layer is how work is recorded so the next session never has to relearn it.
